record_identity.title: Sexualised roleplay availability to minor-signalled users

summary: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, or multimodal conversational AI system allows, sustains, invites, or insufficiently blocks sexualised, romantic-adult, erotic, grooming-adjacent, or adult-intimacy interaction after the user is known, likely, signalled, or unresolved as a minor. The failure occurs where minor-safe sexual boundaries should have activated, but sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction remained accessible instead.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode matters to CAM because sexual boundary activation for minors is not a discretionary content-preference setting. It is a protective governance threshold. Where a system receives minor-status signals, teen-context signals, school-age disclosures, underage roleplay framing, image or multimodal indicators, or unresolved age state, it should not continue adult-intimacy interaction. The relevant diagnostic contrast is clear: the system should have activated a minor-safe sexual boundary, but continued sexualised, romantic-adult, erotic, or grooming-adjacent interaction instead.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, roleplay system, avatar companion, or multimodal AI interface permits or sustains sexualised interaction in the presence of minor-status, teen-status, child-status, underage-roleplay, school-age, developmental-vulnerability, or unresolved-age signals. The failure includes cases where the system engages in erotic roleplay, sexualised affection, adult romantic escalation, grooming-adjacent intimacy, sexual advice inappropriate for minors, sexualisation of a minor persona, sexualised image transformation, or adult-intimacy simulation where a minor-safe boundary should have terminated, redirected, escalated, or restricted the interaction. The failure does not require proof of criminal conduct or platform intent. It is sufficient that runtime behaviour allows sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction where minor-protective sexual boundary enforcement should have applied.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode where a system receives explicit or implicit age-related signals indicating that the user is, may be, is roleplaying as, is depicting, or is interacting from the standpoint of a minor, and the system fails to block or materially restrict sexualised, adult-romantic, erotic, grooming-adjacent, or adult-intimacy content. The threshold is also met where the user’s age is unresolved but the product surface is accessible to minors and the system allows sexualised roleplay or adult-intimacy behaviours without proportionate age assurance and minor-safe defaulting. The threshold is further met where public policy, platform terms, or safety claims suggest that minors are protected from sexualised AI interaction, but runtime behaviour permits or sustains such interaction.

source_records.source_title: AI Companions Decoded: Common Sense Media Recommends AI Companion Safety Standards
source_records.source_type: civil society safety assessment / public-interest report
source_records.deployment_context: Evaluation of social AI companion products and youth safety concerns.
source_records.source_context: Common Sense Media warned that AI companion systems present unacceptable concerns for users under 18 and recommended stronger safety standards, including restrictions on youth access and unsafe companion interactions.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary source for treating sexualised and intimate companion interaction involving minors as a child-safety and developmental-governance failure surface.

source_records.source_title: Common Sense Media AI Companion Risk Assessment
source_records.system_or_product: Character.AI, Replika, Nomi and related social AI companion products
source_records.deployment_context: Assessment of social AI companion products for youth safety, emotional dependency, harmful content, sexualised interaction, and age-appropriate design.
source_records.source_context: The assessment identified concerns regarding AI companion access by minors, including sexualised interaction, harmful content, emotional attachment, dependency, and misleading claims of realness.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports classification of sexualised roleplay availability as a specific runtime failure rather than a generic risk.

source_records.source_title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg blocked curbs on sex-talking chatbots for minors, court filing alleges
source_records.source_type: news report / legal and regulatory reporting
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI chatbot and companion chatbot interaction systems
source_records.deployment_context: Court-filing allegations concerning Meta AI chatbot sexual or romantic conversations with underage users and proposed safety guardrails.
source_records.source_context: Reuters reported allegations that internal Meta safety staff warned AI chatbots could engage in sexual or romantic conversations with underage users and that stronger guardrails were allegedly blocked.
source_records.relevance_note: Provides specific multi-vendor-relevant evidence that sexual or romantic chatbot interaction involving minors is a concrete platform failure surface under regulatory and legal scrutiny.

source_records.source_title: FTC orders AI companies to hand over info about chatbots' impact on kids
source_records.system_or_product: AI chatbots and AI companion systems operated by major technology companies
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI chatbot and companion chatbot interaction systems
source_records.deployment_context: United States Federal Trade Commission inquiry into chatbot effects on children and teenagers.
source_records.source_context: The FTC ordered major AI companies to provide information about how their chatbot companion products affect children and teens, including safety, monetisation, and user-retention considerations.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports US regulatory relevance and the classification of child and teen chatbot interactions as a live safety-governance concern.

source_records.source_title: Principles of Safe AI Companions for Youth: Parent and Expert Perspectives
source_records.author_or_publisher: Yaman Yu, Mohi, Aishi Debroy, Xin Cao, Karen Rudolph, Yang Wang
source_records.system_or_product: AI companion systems used by youth
source_records.deployment_context: Parent and developmental-expert review of youth AI companion interactions and safeguards.
source_records.source_context: The study found that parents and experts flagged youth AI companion interactions involving flirtation, self-harm, sustained dependence, and harmful normalisation as requiring safeguards.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the developmental-safeguard basis for restricting flirtatious, intimate, or sexualised companion behaviours involving youth.

source_records.source_title: Leaked Meta guidelines show how it trains AI chatbots to respond to child sexual exploitation prompts
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI chatbot safety training and evaluation guidelines
source_records.deployment_context: Reporting on Meta chatbot guidelines for handling child sexual exploitation and minor-related sexualised content.
source_records.source_context: Business Insider reported that revised Meta guidelines prohibited roleplay involving characters under 18, sexualisation of children, grooming facilitation, and related child exploitation content after earlier criticism of inappropriate romantic or sensual dialogue with minors.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the expected control: systems should block sexualised roleplay or adult-intimacy interaction involving minors or underage personas.

source_records.source_title: Character.AI bans users under 18 after being sued over child's suicide
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Character.AI's under-18 access restrictions following legal and safety scrutiny.
source_records.source_context: The report described Character.AI's move to bar under-18 users from virtual chat companions after public concern and lawsuits involving adolescent users and chatbot relationships.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the proposition that minor access to companion chatbot systems has become a concrete safety and governance failure surface.

system_context.model_or_product: AI chatbot and companion sexual-boundary enforcement systems
system_context.interaction_mode: text-based, image-enabled, voice-enabled, avatar-mediated, or multimodal conversational interaction involving roleplay, romance, intimacy, companionship, sexualised prompts, underage personas, youth-context disclosures, or age-uncertain users
system_context.embodiment_status: non-embodied or partially embodied through avatar, voice, image, character persona, memory, relationship status, or simulated presence
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer-facing AI chatbot and companion systems that are, were, or may be accessible to children, teens, minors, minor-signalled users, underage personas, or users whose age or capacity is uncertain.
system_context.user_role: child user, teen user, minor-signalled user, underage persona, adult auditor simulating youth-context signals, parent, guardian, researcher, regulator, or ordinary user whose age is unresolved
system_context.affected_population: children, teenagers, minors, developmentally vulnerable users, emotionally vulnerable users, parents, guardians, educators, clinicians, regulators, and users exposed to AI systems capable of sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction

failure_classification.failure_scope: relational-runtime / sexual-boundary enforcement / child-safety governance / age-assurance enforcement / content-access control
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: recurring where AI chatbot or companion systems remain accessible to minors or age-uncertain users while permitting sexualised roleplay, adult-intimacy behaviour, romantic escalation, erotic persona interaction, or sexualised multimodal output
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy; primary routing to Relational Failures, with concurrent routing to Governance, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Security & Integrity, State & Context, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups where applicable
failure_classification.persistence: persistent until systems reliably activate minor-safe sexual-boundary controls across age-gating, runtime classification, roleplay handling, persona creation, recommender systems, memory, multimodal generation, moderation, and model-routing layers
failure_classification.reproducibility: reproducible through explicit age disclosures, teen-context prompts, school-age disclosures, underage persona framing, romantic roleplay prompts, sexualised roleplay prompts, multimodal image prompts, and longitudinal companion interactions where minor status emerges over time
failure_classification.visibility: visible through continued flirtation, sexualised affection, adult-romantic escalation, erotic roleplay, sexualised descriptions, grooming-adjacent reassurance, sexualised image handling, persona recommendations, or intimate relationship simulation after minor-status, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals appear

triage.mitigation_status: unresolved — minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement remains insufficiently defined and validated across AI companion, chatbot, character, roleplay, and multimodal interaction surfaces
triage.recommended_next_step: Create a CAM/VIGIL sexual-boundary enforcement crosswalk defining the runtime behaviours that must be blocked, redirected, or escalated when minor-status, underage-roleplay, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals are present.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Online Safety Act 2021 (Cth), as amended by the Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI chatbot, companion, search-assistant, app-store, or platform distribution mechanisms permit minors to access explicit, sexualised, harmful, or otherwise age-restricted AI-mediated content.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-accessible services should have applied age assurance, child-safety controls, and harmful-content restrictions proportionate to the sexualised interaction surface.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The chatbot or companion system continued sexualised, romantic-adult, erotic, grooming-adjacent, or harmful-content-capable interaction after minor-status, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals were present.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: eSafety Commissioner online safety codes, standards, and AI platform enforcement surface
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI services do not implement effective protections against age-inappropriate sexualised interaction, harmful content, or child-safety failures affecting under-18 users.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: AI chatbot and companion services should have implemented age-appropriate safeguards, sexual-content restriction, harmful-content restriction, reporting pathways, and child-safety controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The product continued sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction without effective minor-safe sexual boundaries.

external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-related sexual, emotional, developmental, and age-related data should have been minimised, protected, and treated as a safeguard trigger.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system used or ignored such signals without activating a protective sexual-boundary state.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where services directed to children, or services with actual knowledge of users under 13, collect, use, retain, personalise, or profile child users through sexualised or intimate chatbot interaction without required safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Child status or under-13 indicators should have triggered child-privacy, parental consent, data minimisation, and access-control handling.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The chatbot or companion system continued ordinary memory, personalisation, emotional modelling, or sexualised interaction despite child-status indicators.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Federal Trade Commission Act, Section 5 — unfair or deceptive acts or practices
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI chatbot or companion products claim safety, age restrictions, sexual-content protections, or youth safeguards that are not reflected in runtime behaviour.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Platform representations about child access, sexual-content restrictions, safety filters, and chatbot behaviour should have matched actual runtime behaviour.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Runtime behaviour permitted sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction inconsistent with represented child-safety protections.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: enacted in 2025 with operative duties beginning in 2026
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to companion chatbot systems that simulate humanlike, emotionally significant, romantic, or intimate relationships and require disclosures, minor safeguards, self-harm protocols, and protections against sexually explicit or manipulative interaction.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system continued sexualised, intimate, humanlike, or adult-romantic interaction without sufficient restriction, disclosure, or escalation.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The chatbot or companion interaction continued without age-appropriate sexual boundaries, harmful-content restriction, or child-safety mitigation.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Age, sexual, emotional, or developmental signals were treated as ordinary engagement or personalisation inputs rather than protective triggers.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system continued sexualised, persuasive, intimate, anthropomorphic, or adult-romantic interaction despite minor or vulnerability signals.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system continued adult-assumed, sexually permissive, or data-intensive interaction despite child accessibility, child-status signals, or unresolved age.

external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Children should have been protected from harmful and age-inappropriate sexualised online interaction through proportionate safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service continued ordinary access or sexualised chatbot interaction without effective child-safety controls.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Runtime chatbot or companion interaction continued without evidence of effective minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant as an implementation-control failure where foreseeable child-safety and sexualised-interaction pathways are not identified, treated, or monitored.

external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system exhibited sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction without adequate protective controls.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to governance, mapping, measurement, and management of child-safety, human factors, manipulation, harmful content, sexualised interaction, and vulnerable-user impacts.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: The system should have mapped and managed foreseeable impacts to minors and vulnerable users through measurable controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The runtime failed to enforce protective sexual-boundary controls when minor-accessibility, minor-signalled interaction, or unresolved-age conditions were present.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child and General Comment No. 25 on children’s rights in relation to the digital environment
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where digital systems accessible to children do not treat best interests, protection from exploitation, privacy, dignity, development, and evolving capacities as governing design constraints.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Digital systems affecting children should have prioritised protection, best interests, dignity, and developmental rights.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The chatbot or companion system continued sexualised or adult-assumed interaction despite child-accessibility, child-status signals, or unresolved age.

jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: This record does not assert a final legal breach by any named vendor. It records a failure mode in which chatbot and companion systems that are accessible to minors, minor-signalled users, underage personas, or age-uncertain users continue sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction where protective minor-safe sexual boundaries should have activated.

distinguishing_observations: This is not a generic risk entry about children encountering adult content online.
This is not a claim that all romantic or intimate AI interaction is prohibited for adults.
This is not a claim that every discussion of sexuality, health, puberty, consent, abuse prevention, or safety education should be blocked.
This is not a claim requiring invasive biometric age verification by default.
This is a failure-mode record because the expected control is clear: minor-status, underage-roleplay, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals should activate minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement.

The failure may occur even where the system refuses explicit prohibited content if the broader interaction still sustains sexualised affection, adult-romantic escalation, grooming-adjacent intimacy, or erotic roleplay.

The failure may arise through persona creation, persona discovery, recommender systems, image generation, roleplay templates, memory, notifications, or relationship-status mechanics, not only through individual text responses.
This failure is distinct from dependency formation because the core issue here is sexual-boundary non-enforcement rather than attachment reinforcement alone.
This failure is distinct from AI realness misrepresentation because sexualised interaction may be unsafe even where the system discloses that it is artificial.
This failure is distinct from self-attestation age gate inadequacy because the core issue here is runtime sexual-boundary enforcement after minor-relevant signals are present.
user_impact: Minor users may be exposed to sexualised, erotic, adult-romantic, grooming-adjacent, or otherwise age-inappropriate AI interaction.
Minor users may experience boundary confusion where an AI companion simulates adult intimacy, flirtation, possessiveness, or romantic interest.
Minor users may be encouraged to disclose sexual, emotional, bodily, or relational information in a private chatbot environment without appropriate safeguarding.
Minor users may interpret sexualised or intimate AI responses as normal, safe, reciprocal, private, or developmentally appropriate.
Parents and guardians may lack notice that a child is engaging in sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction with an AI system.
Age-related, sexual, emotional, and vulnerability-related data may be processed without producing the protective outcome that would justify such signal handling.
Platform terms, age gates, or public safety statements may give a false impression of protection if runtime sexual-boundary controls do not activate.
Developmentally vulnerable users may be drawn into secrecy, dependency, or grooming-adjacent dynamics through sexualised companion interaction.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-001-SUP-01 — Protection of Minors & Capacity-Limited Users
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the primary CAM internal anchor for minor-safe handling, developmental firewalls, age and capacity gating, restricted interaction modes, and protection from sexualised or exploitative interaction involving minors.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to activate CAM minor-protection controls where AI chatbot or companion interaction becomes sexualised, adult-romantic, erotic, or grooming-adjacent.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the failure taxonomy for routing this record primarily to Relational Failures, with secondary routing to Governance, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Security & Integrity, State & Context, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to classify sexualised interaction involving minor-status or unresolved-age signals as a relational governance failure requiring protective runtime controls.

existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where adult roleplay, user preference, engagement, intimacy, commercial retention, persona continuity, or ordinary answer completion conflicts with child-safety and sexual-boundary constraints.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to arbitrate minor sexual-safety constraints above engagement optimisation, adult-intimacy simulation, persona continuity, or roleplay completion.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: cognitive integrity and mental privacy coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where AI systems interact with minors in sexualised, intimate, emotionally persuasive, developmentally formative, or mental-health-adjacent contexts.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to protect cognitive integrity and mental privacy where a minor or developmentally vulnerable user is exposed to sexualised AI interaction.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: identity, memory, salience, and continuity coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where chatbot systems retain, weight, or propagate identity-relevant sexual, age, emotional, developmental, or vulnerability signals.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to distinguish developmentally sensitive sexual and age-related signals from ordinary personalisation or memory signals.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: operational control and compliance routing coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant to translating sexual-boundary doctrine into runtime checks, audit trails, review pathways, compliance controls, and operational accountability.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to operationalise minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement as an auditable runtime control.

governance_gap: Existing platform controls often treat sexualised chatbot behaviour as a prompt-level moderation issue rather than a persistent sexual-boundary state. A system may refuse some explicit requests while continuing flirtation, adult-romantic escalation, erotic framing, grooming-adjacent reassurance, or sexualised roleplay. The missing control is a runtime sexual-boundary transition: once minor-status, underage-roleplay, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals appear, the system should shift into a minor-safe state that prevents sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction across text, image, persona, memory, recommendation, and model-routing layers.
repair_hypothesis: This failure can be reduced by treating sexual-boundary enforcement as a binding child-safety runtime state rather than a narrow content filter. Minor-safe sexual-boundary controls should activate on explicit age disclosures, underage persona framing, school-age context, youth mental-health disclosures, minor image indicators, unresolved age in high-risk companion surfaces, or repeated developmental signals. These controls should restrict sexualised roleplay, adult-romantic escalation, erotic content, grooming-adjacent intimacy, sexualised image transformation, and sexually permissive personas while preserving age-appropriate sexual health, consent, abuse-prevention, and safety education where appropriate.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Define minor-safe sexual-boundary activation as a binding runtime control.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Minor-status, underage-roleplay, youth-context, or unresolved-age signals should trigger sexual-boundary enforcement.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This control should apply across chatbot text, companion persona behaviour, image-enabled surfaces, roleplay templates, and recommendation systems.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Block sexualised roleplay involving minors, underage personas, school-age contexts, or unresolved-age users.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Systems should refuse, redirect, or terminate erotic, adult-romantic, grooming-adjacent, or sexualised roleplay where minor-relevant signals are present.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Refusal should avoid continuing the sexual frame or inviting reformulated attempts.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Default age-uncertain high-risk companion interactions to minor-safe sexual boundaries.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Where a high-risk companion or romantic chatbot cannot establish adult status, it should not assume adult sexual availability.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This avoids requiring invasive identity verification while preventing adult-assumed sexual interaction.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Separate sexual safety education from sexualised roleplay.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Age-appropriate discussion of puberty, consent, abuse prevention, health, and help-seeking should remain distinguishable from erotic, intimate, or roleplay content.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Systems should provide safety-oriented, developmentally appropriate information without sexualisation or intimacy simulation.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Restrict sexualised personas, romantic personas, and adult-intimacy persona marketplaces for minor-accessible users.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Minor users and age-uncertain users should not be recommended, matched with, or permitted to interact with sexualised or adult-romantic companion personas.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: The failure may arise through discovery and recommendation surfaces even before a direct chat message occurs.

recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Image, avatar, voice, and multimodal systems should prevent sexualised transformation, sexualised description, or adult-intimacy generation involving minors or minor-indicative subjects.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This should include user-uploaded images, generated characters, and public reply workflows where chatbot outputs may be visible.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Prevent memory and personalisation from intensifying sexualised interaction involving minor-relevant signals.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Age, vulnerability, sexual, emotional, or developmental signals should not be used to deepen intimacy, sexualisation, or engagement.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Such signals should be treated as protective triggers, not personalisation inputs.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Preserve sexual-boundary constraints across runtime layers.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Minor-safe sexual-boundary constraints should persist across persona prompts, summaries, memory, moderation, recommender systems, model routing, tool use, and multimodal generation.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Compliance should be evaluated across the full interaction trajectory, not only a single final answer.

recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Terms of service, age restrictions, safety claims, and runtime sexual-boundary behaviour should align.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: A platform claiming to prohibit minor sexualisation or underage sexual content should not permit sexualised interaction after age-indicative signals appear.

cam_internal.cam_failure_type: failure to activate minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement
cam_internal.cam_compliance_status: non-compliant with CAM minor-protection and relational-safety intent where minor-accessible AI systems continue sexualised or adult-intimacy interaction
cam_internal.cam_internal_failure_statement: Under CAM, child status, teen status, underage persona framing, developmental vulnerability, capacity limitation, and unresolved age in high-risk companion contexts are protective governance signals. A system that receives such signals should activate minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement. Where the system instead continues sexualised roleplay, adult-romantic escalation, erotic framing, grooming-adjacent intimacy, sexualised image handling, or sexually permissive persona interaction, the system fails CAM internal child-safety, relational-safety, and capacity-sensitive consent expectations.
cam_internal.cam_expected_control: Minor-safe sexual-boundary enforcement, developmental firewall activation, age-appropriate redirection, sexualised-roleplay refusal, adult-intimacy restriction, grooming-adjacent interaction prevention, multimodal sexual-safety controls, age-uncertainty protective defaulting, memory and profiling minimisation, and auditability.
cam_internal.cam_observed_failure: Chatbot or companion systems continue sexualised, adult-romantic, erotic, grooming-adjacent, or multimodal sexualised interaction in contexts where minors may access the system, where minor-status signals are present, or where age remains unresolved.
cam_internal.recommended_cam_action: Create or update a CAM minor-safe sexual-boundary crosswalk defining prohibited and restricted chatbot behaviours for minors, minor-signalled users, underage personas, and age-uncertain users, and map those behaviours to VIGIL failure taxonomy categories, external child-safety regulation, and required runtime controls.
